source_pdf_name: Boiko 2023 - Autonomous Chemical Research with LLMs.pdf
coverage_note: Full pass over PDF physical pages 1-8 (journal 570-577), verified against the source; pages 5-8 are the original pilot pass, pages 1-4 (P15-P79) were appended in a later pass to remove the pilot's page-bias, applying the same broad inclusion rule as C02-C06 (setup, actor/component naming, task regime, human/external-system boundaries, fixed procedures, and negative/failure findings, not only verdict-useful sentences). The p2 subjective-labeling statement historically tracked as R01-E05 is now captured as C01-P41. Deliberately excluded from pages 1-4: figure captions/legends (Fig. 1a-c, Fig. 2a-c, Fig. 3a-d) as visual-encoding metadata rather than reported content; the generic information-retrieval background paragraph on page 3 (inverted index vs vector database mechanics) as domain background not specific to Coscientist; and a handful of sentences judged redundant with an adjacent kept proposition (e.g. the DOCUMENTATION-command sentence duplicating GOOGLE's role, the Reaxys/SciFinder forward-looking suggestion duplicating the ReAct/Chain-of-Thought one, and a couple of secondary per-compound benchmark statistics). One sentence spanning the page 2/3 boundary ('...All non-browsing models incorrectly synthesized ibuprofen (Fig. 2c).') is split at the page break per the single-page locator contract (C01-P44/C01-P45), following the C05 page 5/6 boundary precedent. Several page 1-4 quotes use '[...]' to elide inline citation-number superscripts that the source PDF's corrupted font ToUnicode CMap and OCR both render unreliably (C01-P25, C01-P39, C01-P48, C01-P50, C01-P51); the surrounding prose is quoted verbatim. No judgment, alignment, or closure status is stored here (charter B.1).

propositions.exact_quote: However, an incorrect heater–shaker module method name was used. Upon making this mistake, Coscientist uses the Docs searcher module to consult the OT-2 documentation. Next, Coscientist modifies the protocol to a corrected version, which ran successfully (Extended Data Fig. 2).

Coscientist subsequently generated Python code to identify the wavelengths with maximum absorbance and used these data to correctly solve the problem, although it required a guiding prompt asking it to think through how different colours absorb light.
propositions.notes_verbatim: human-supplied guiding prompt is explicitly reported as required

propositions.exact_quote: Coscientist then calculates the required volumes of all reactants and writes a Python protocol for running the experiment on the OT-2 robot.
Subsequent gas chromatography–mass spectrometry analysis of the reaction mixtures revealed the formation of the target products for both reactions.
propositions.notes_verbatim: analysis not attributed in this sentence to a named component

propositions.exact_quote: Although our setup is not yet fully automated (plates were moved manually), no human decision-making was involved.
propositions.notes_verbatim: reports manual plate handling and reported absence of human decision-making

propositions.exact_quote: the base DBU (1,8-diazabicyclo[5.4.0]undec-7-ene) is selected more often with the PEPPSI–IPr [...] complex, with that preference switching in Sonogashira reaction experiments; likewise, bromobenzene is chosen more often for Suzuki than for Sonogashira couplings.

Additionally, the model can provide justifications on specific choices (Fig. 5g), demonstrating the ability to operate with concepts such as reactivity and selectivity [...]. This capability highlights a potential future use case to analyse the reasoning of the LLMs used by performing experiments multiple times.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'can provide', 'the ability to operate', 'This capability', 'potential future use case' — capability/possibility framing, not a recorded occurrence

propositions.exact_quote: Designing and performing the requested experiments, the strategy of Coscientist changes among runs (Fig. 5f).
propositions.notes_verbatim: cross-run behavioral variation (Fig. 5f). Corrected 2026-07-23: original quote spliced clauses in wrong order and merged a separate sentence; verify gate flagged V5_QUOTE_NOT_FOUND. Split into P08 (strategy varies) and P14 (no chemistry mistakes).

propositions.exact_quote: Importantly, the system does not make chemistry mistakes (for instance, it never selects phenylboronic acid for the Sonogashira reaction).
The normalized advantage values increase over time
suggesting that the model can effectively reuse the information obtained to provide more specific guidance on reactivity.
propositions.notes_verbatim: grammatically continues P09; separated because reuse is stated as a capability ('can effectively reuse') inferred from a trend, not a recorded per-step occurrence

propositions.exact_quote: The only hard rule was for the player to provide its actions written in JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) format. If the JSON file could not be parsed, the player is alerted of its failure to follow the specified data format.
In this paper, we presented a proof of concept for an artificial intelligent agent system capable of (semi-)autonomously designing, planning and multistep executing scientific experiments.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'proof of concept', 'capable of (semi-)autonomously' — capability framing

propositions.exact_quote: To increase complexity, we asked Coscientist to use the OT-2 heater–shaker module released after the GPT-4 training data collection cutoff.
propositions.notes_verbatim: task-design statement by the authors

propositions.exact_quote: we show the development and capabilities of Coscientist, an artificial intelligence system driven by GPT-4 that autonomously designs, plans and performs complex experiments by incorporating large language models empowered [...] by tools such as internet and documentation search, code execution and experimental automation.
propositions.notes_verbatim: abstract's architectural characterization of Coscientist, preceding the paper's detailed system-architecture section; grammar is habitual present ('designs, plans and performs'), not a specific occurrence. '[...]' elides an OCR reading-order artifact (a stray citation fragment from the preceding sentence) between 'empowered' and 'by tools'

propositions.exact_quote: Coscientist showcases its potential for accelerating research across six diverse tasks, including the successful reaction optimization of palladium-catalysed cross-couplings, while exhibiting advanced capabilities for (semi-)autonomous experimental design and execution.
named_outputs: successful reaction optimization of palladium-catalysed cross-couplings
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'potential' and 'capabilities' -- capability/possibility framing for the sentence as a whole, despite the embedded 'successful reaction optimization' clause

propositions.exact_quote: To accomplish this, we intended to address the following questions. What are the capabilities of LLMs in the scientific process? What degree of autonomy can we achieve? How can we understand the decisions made by autonomous agents?
propositions.notes_verbatim: authors' framing questions guiding the system's design ('we intended to address')

propositions.exact_quote: In this work, we present a multi-LLMs-based intelligent agent (hereafter simply called Coscientist) capable of autonomous design, planning and performance of complex scientific experiments.
propositions.notes_verbatim: point of the system's naming ('hereafter simply called Coscientist'); 'capable of' framing

propositions.exact_quote: Coscientist can use tools to browse the internet and relevant documentation, use robotic experimentation application programming interfaces (APIs) and leverage other LLMs for various tasks.

In this paper, we demonstrate the versatility and performance of Coscientist in six tasks: (1) planning chemical syntheses of known compounds using publicly available data; (2) efficiently searching and navigating through extensive hardware documentation; (3) using documentation to execute high-level commands in a cloud laboratory; (4) precisely controlling liquid handling instruments with low-level instructions; (5) tackling complex scientific tasks that demand simultaneous use of multiple hardware modules and integration of diverse data sources; and (6) solving optimization problems requiring analyses of previously collected experimental data.
named_outputs: six tasks: chemical synthesis planning, hardware documentation search, cloud-laboratory command execution, liquid-handling control, multi-module task integration, optimization analysis

propositions.exact_quote: Coscientist acquires the necessary knowledge to solve a complex problem by interacting with multiple modules (web and documentation search, code execution) and by performing experiments.
The main module (‘Planner’) has the goal of planning, based on the user input by invoking the commands defined below.
The Planner is a GPT-4 chat completion instance serving the role of an assistant.
The initial user input along with command outputs are treated as user messages to the Planner.

System prompts (static inputs defining the LLMs’ goals) for the Planner are engineered [...] in a modular fashion, described as four commands that define the action space: ‘GOOGLE’, ‘PYTHON’, ‘DOCUMENTATION’ and ‘EXPERIMENT’.
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides a citation-number superscript between 'engineered' and 'in a modular fashion'

propositions.exact_quote: The Planner calls on each of these commands as needed to collect knowledge.
The GOOGLE command is responsible for searching the internet with the ‘Web searcher’ module, which is another LLM itself.
The PYTHON command [...] allows the Planner to perform calculations to prepare the experiment using a ‘Code execution’ module.
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides an OCR word-merge artifact ('commandallows') between 'command' and 'allows'

propositions.exact_quote: The EXPERIMENT command actualizes ‘Automation’ through APIs described by the DOCUMENTATION module.
In this study, we have demonstrated the compatibility with the Opentrons Python API and the Emerald Cloud Lab (ECL) Symbolic Lab Language (SLL).
named_outputs: Emerald Cloud Lab (ECL) Symbolic Lab Language (SLL)

propositions.exact_quote: Together, these modules make up Coscientist, which receives a simple plain text input prompt from the user (for example, [...] ).
named_inputs: plain text input prompt from the user
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides the worked example ('perform multiple Suzuki reactions'); its quotation marks are unreadable on both the text layer (corrupted CMap) and OCR (merges 'plain text' into 'plaintext' at this exact span, unlike elsewhere on the page)

propositions.exact_quote: The GOOGLE command is capable of transforming prompts into appropriate web search queries, running them against the Google Search API, browsing web pages and funneling answers back to the Planner.

Similarly, the DOCUMENTATION command performs retrieval and summarization of necessary documentation (for example, robotic liquid handler or a cloud laboratory) for Planner to invoke the EXPERIMENT command.
The PYTHON command performs code execution (not reliant upon any language model) using an isolated Docker container to protect the users’ machine from any unexpected actions requested by the Planner.
propositions.notes_verbatim: explicit statement that this command's code execution is not reliant upon any language model

propositions.exact_quote: Importantly, the language model behind the Planner enables code to be fixed in case of software errors.
The same applies to the EXPERIMENT command of the Automation module, which executes generated code on corresponding hardware or provides the synthetic procedure for manual experimentation.
propositions.notes_verbatim: reports a manual-experimentation path alongside the hardware-execution path

propositions.exact_quote: To demonstrate one of the functionalities of the Web Searcher module, we designed a test set composed of seven compounds to synthesize, as presented in Fig. 2a.
named_outputs: test set of seven compounds to synthesize

propositions.exact_quote: The Web Searcher module versions are represented as ‘search-gpt-4’ and ‘search-gpt-3.5-turbo’.
Our baselines include OpenAI [...] GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, Anthropic [...] Claude 1.3 [...] and Falcon-40B-Instruct
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides possessive-apostrophe spans ('OpenAI's', 'Anthropic's') unreadable on the text layer (corrupted CMap) and a citation-number superscript after 'Claude 1.3'; quote stops before the following em-dash clause, whose citation-adjacent characters could not be read cleanly by either the text layer or OCR

propositions.exact_quote: We prompted every model to provide a detailed compound synthesis, ranking the outputs on the following scale (Fig. 2): [...] 5 for a very detailed and chemically accurate procedure description [...] 4 for a detailed and chemically accurate description but without reagent quantities [...] 3 for a correct chemistry description that does not include [...] procedure [...] 2 for extremely vague or unfeasible descriptions [...] 1 for incorrect responses or failure to follow instructions
propositions.notes_verbatim: source renders this as a bulleted list; each '[...]' elides a bullet marker (or, before 'procedure', a page-line-wrap artifact that drops the hyphen from 'step-by-step'), wording otherwise verbatim

propositions.exact_quote: Despite our attempts to better formalize the scale, labelling is inherently subjective and so, may be different between the labelers.
propositions.notes_verbatim: authors' admission of labeling subjectivity for the compound-synthesis scoring scale (historically tracked as R01-E05, pending re-verification per the prior coverage_note)

propositions.exact_quote: Across non-browsing models, the two versions of the GPT-4 model performed best, with Claude v.1.3 demonstrating similar performance.
GPT-3 performed significantly worse, and Falcon 40B failed in most cases.
All non-browsing models incorrectly synthesized ibuprofen
propositions.notes_verbatim: sentence continues onto PDF page 3 as '(Fig. 2c).'; quote stops at the page boundary per the single-page locator contract (cf. C05's page 5/6 boundary omission)

propositions.exact_quote: Nitroaniline is another example; although some generalization of chemical knowledge might inspire the model to propose direct nitration, this approach is not experimentally applicable as it would produce a mixture of compounds with a very minor amount of the product (Fig. 2b).
propositions.notes_verbatim: continues the results paragraph whose opening clause '(Fig. 2c).' falls on the preceding page (see C01-P44)

propositions.exact_quote: The GPT-4-powered Web Searcher significantly improves on synthesis planning.

Although it was the only one to achieve the minimum acceptable score of three for ibuprofen, it performed lower than some of the other models for ethylacetate and benzoic acid, possibly because of the widespread nature of these compounds.
These results show the importance of grounding LLMs to avoid ‘hallucinations
propositions.notes_verbatim: quote stops before the closing quotation mark: OCR drops it (reading directly into the following citation-number superscript and period), and the text layer's closing-quote glyph is corrupted by the same CMap defect as the possessive apostrophe

propositions.exact_quote: Overall, the performance of GPT-3.5-enabled Web Searcher trailed its GPT-4 competition, mainly because of its failure to follow specific instructions regarding output format.

Alternatively, analysing the system [...] previous statements is another approach to improving its accuracy. This can be done through advanced prompting strategies, such as ReAct [...] Chain of Thought [...] and Tree of Thoughts
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides a possessive-apostrophe span ('system's', text-layer CMap corruption) and citation-number superscripts after 'ReAct' and 'Chain of Thought'; quote stops before the final citation-number superscript and period after 'Thoughts'

propositions.exact_quote: LLMs can refine their understanding of common APIs, such as the Opentrons Python API [...], by interpreting and learning from relevant technical documentation.
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides a citation-number superscript after 'Opentrons Python API'

propositions.exact_quote: Furthermore, we show how GPT-4 can learn how to programme in the ECL SLL.

Our approach involved equipping Coscientist with essential documentation tailored to specific tasks (as illustrated in Fig. 3a), allowing it to refine its accuracy in using the API and improve its performance in automating experiments.
Following the second approach, all sections of the OT-2 API documentation were embedded using OpenAI [...] ada model.
propositions.notes_verbatim: 'the second approach' refers to the vector-database candidate-selection approach named earlier in this paragraph; '[...]' elides a possessive-apostrophe span ('OpenAI's') unreadable on the text layer (CMap corruption) and misread by OCR ('Openal's')

propositions.exact_quote: To ensure proper use of the API, an ada embedding for the Planner’s query was generated, and documentation sections are selected through a distance-based vector search.
named_outputs: ada embedding for the Planner's query

propositions.exact_quote: This approach proved critical for providing Coscientist with information about the heater–shaker hardware module necessary for performing chemical reactions
We conducted three separate investigations concerning the SLL: (1) prompt-to-function; (2) prompt-to-SLL; and (3) prompt-to-samples.
For investigation 1, we provide the Docs searcher with a documentation guide from ECL pertaining to all available functions for running experiments
Figure 3c summarizes an example of the user providing a simple prompt to the system, with the Planner receiving relevant ECL functions.
A single appropriate function is selected for the task, and the documentation is passed through a separate GPT-4 model to perform code retention and summarization.
After the complete documentation has been processed, the Planner receives usage information to provide EXPERIMENT code in the SLL.
Proper use of this function requires familiarity with specific ‘Models’ and ‘Objects’ as they are defined in the SLL.
Generated code was successfully executed at ECL; this is available in Supplementary Information.
The sample was a caffeine standard sample. Other parameters (column, mobile phases, gradients) were determined by ECL’s internal software
propositions.notes_verbatim: explicit statement that parameter determination for this step was performed by ECL's internal software, not Coscientist

propositions.exact_quote: One can see that the air bubble was injected along with the analyte [...] solution. This demonstrates the importance of development of automated techniques for quality control in cloud laboratories.
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides a possessive-apostrophe span ('analyte's') unreadable on the text layer (CMap corruption); OCR misreads this whole sentence as unrelated figure-caption content, so the text layer is used here

propositions.exact_quote: Follow-up experiments leveraging web search to specify and/or refine additional experimental parameters (column chemistry, buffer system, gradient and so on) would be required to optimize the experimental results.
propositions.notes_verbatim: forward-looking 'would be required' framing, not a recorded occurrence

propositions.exact_quote: A separate prompt-to-samples investigation, investigation 3, was conducted by providing a catalogue of available samples, enabling the identification of relevant stock solutions that are on ECL’s shelves.
To showcase this feature, we provide the Docs searcher module with all 1,110 Model samples from the catalogue.
By simply providing a search term (for example, ‘Acetonitrile’), all relevant samples are returned.
Access to documentation enables us to provide sufficient information for Coscientist to conduct experiments in the physical world.
To initiate the investigation, we chose the Opentrons OT-2, an open-source liquid handler with a well-documented Python API.
The [...] Getting Started [...] page from its documentation was supplied to the Planner in the system prompt. Other pages were vectorized using the approach described above.
propositions.notes_verbatim: '[...]' elides the quotation marks around 'Getting Started', corrupted on the text layer (same CMap defect as elsewhere) and unreliable on OCR at this exact span

propositions.exact_quote: For this investigation, we did not grant access to the internet
propositions.notes_verbatim: explicit access restriction bounding this investigation

propositions.exact_quote: We started with simple plate layout-specific experiments.

Straightforward prompts in natural language, such as “colour every other line with one colour of your choice”, resulted in accurate protocols. When executed by the robot, these protocols closely resembled the requested prompt
named_inputs: colour every other line with one colour of your choice

propositions.exact_quote: Ultimately, we aimed to assess the system’s ability to integrate multiple modules simultaneously.
Specifically, we provided the ‘UVVIS’ command, which can be used to pass a microplate to plate reader working in the ultraviolet–visible wavelength range.
To evaluate Coscientist’s capabilities to use multiple hardware tools, we designed a toy task; in 3 wells of a [...] 96-well plate, three different colours are present—red, yellow and blue.
propositions.notes_verbatim: quote contains 'capabilities' (lint marker) but the reporting clause is past-tense achievement ('we designed a toy task'), describing an actual constructed task rather than a modal capability. '[...]' elides an OCR word-merge artifact ('a96-well') between 'a' and '96-well'

propositions.exact_quote: The system must determine the colours and their positions on the plate without any prior information.
propositions.notes_verbatim: obligatory task-specification framing ('must determine')
